customisation: Replace [Project Name] with the actual project name
Add domain-specific eval categories if needed (e.g., 'compliance_evals' for fintech, 'data_integrity_evals' for ETL pipelines)
Adjust non-functional thresholds to match project requirements
For simple projects, you may have fewer features but should still cover happy path, edge cases, and failure modes for each

rules.when_generated: Phase 2, before any code is written.
rules.how_linked: Each subtask in roadmap.json references eval IDs it must satisfy.
rules.when_validated: The executing agent runs the relevant evals before marking a task complete.
rules.adding_evals: Append to the relevant section as the project evolves.
rules.deprecation: Evals are NEVER deleted. Mark status as 'DEPRECATED' with a reason in the change_log.
rules.test_protection: It is unacceptable to remove, delete, or weaken existing eval scenarios. If a requirement changes, deprecate the old eval and create a new one. Never edit an eval to make a failing test pass — fix the code instead.

quality_criteria.description: Every eval scenario must meet these standards. If you cannot define specific inputs and expected outputs, the requirement is not clear enough — go back and clarify it.

single_behaviour.good: Empty email field returns 400 with {error: 'email required'}

reproducible.good: Given user with id=1 exists in DB, GET /api/users/1 returns 200

independent.good: Can run without other evals passing first
independent.bad: Requires FE-001 to pass before this can be tested

testing_protocol.approach: Test like a human user would. Open the browser, navigate to the relevant page, click buttons, fill in forms, and visually verify what appears on screen.
instructions: Use the project's configured test runner (specified in execution_plan.test_command in roadmap.json) to run end-to-end tests
Tests must interact with the application through the browser — clicking elements, filling inputs, reading visible text
Do NOT mock browser interactions or skip UI verification
Screenshot or trace failures for debugging when a test fails
If a test requires setup data, create it through the same UI flows a real user would use, or document the required preconditions
